headline: Anti-corruption court backlog grew to 847 pending cases
context: Malaysia's Special Corruption Court had 847 cases pending as of December 2025, up from 523 in 2023, with average case duration stretching to 4.2 years.

cards.big: 847 corruption cases pending in court — average trial takes 4.2 years
cards.sub: Anti-corruption court backlog grew 62% in two years

cards.big: Only 7 special corruption court judges handle the entire national caseload
cards.sub: Case-to-judge ratio of 121:1 makes timely justice mathematically impossible

cards.big: Delayed corruption cases involve RM8.4 billion in contested assets and funds
cards.sub: 4.2-year average trial means billions remain in legal limbo for years

cards.big: Indonesia's Tipikor courts resolve corruption cases in average 8 months
cards.sub: Dedicated anti-corruption judiciary with adequate resources delivers faster justice

cards.big: Justice delayed in corruption cases isn't just justice denied — it's corruption rewarded with time
Should Malaysia expand the special corruption court to at least 20 judges nationwide?
